action.description: The clap action driving how values are applied (e.g. `Set`, `SetTrue`, `Append`).

default_values.description:
Default values (as shown by clap and used when the argument is absent).

This is always present; an empty list means there is no configured default.

global.description: Whether the argument is global (propagates to subcommands).

hidden.description: Whether the argument is hidden from normal help output.

id.description: Clap argument id (stable identifier used for conflict groups, etc.).

num_args_max.description:
Maximum number of values this argument accepts per occurrence (inclusive).

`None` means unbounded.

num_args_min.description: Minimum number of values this argument accepts per occurrence.

possible_values.description:
Enumerated possible values for the argument value parser (if known).

This is always present; an empty list means clap does not advertise a finite set of
possible values for this argument.

value_enum.description:
Whether the argument's value is parsed as a clap `ValueEnum` type.

This is intended for tooling (e.g., rendering dropdowns) and is a best-effort reflection of
the clap configuration.

root.description: Root command and its full subcommand tree.

after_long_help.description: Extra help appended after long help.

args.description: Arguments available at this command level (including hidden and generated help/version args).

hidden.description: Whether the command is hidden from normal help output.

path.description: Full path from the root command, inclusive.

AgentConfig.description: Agent runner defaults. Built-in runner IDs: codex, opencode, gemini, claude, cursor, kimi, pi. Plugin runner IDs are also supported as non-empty strings.

claude_bin.description: Override the claude executable name/path (default is "claude" if None).

claude_permission_mode.description:
Claude permission mode for tool and edit approval.
AcceptEdits: auto-approves file edits only
BypassPermissions: skip all permission prompts (YOLO mode)

codex_bin.description: Override the codex executable name/path (default is "codex" if None).

cursor_bin.description:
Override the cursor agent executable name/path (default is "agent" if None).

NOTE: Cursor's runner binary name is `agent` (not `cursor`).

followup_reasoning_effort.description:
Reasoning effort override for follow-up iterations (iterations > 1).
Only meaningful for Codex models.

gemini_bin.description: Override the gemini executable name/path (default is "gemini" if None).

git_publish_mode.description: Post-run git publication behavior after successful runs.

git_revert_mode.description: Controls automatic git revert behavior when runner or supervision errors occur.

instruction_files.description:
Additional instruction files to inject at the top of every prompt sent to runner CLIs.

Paths may be absolute, `~/`-prefixed, or repo-root relative. Each list entry must be a
non-empty path; blank or whitespace-only strings are rejected during config validation.
Missing files are treated as configuration errors. To include repo-local AGENTS.md, add
`"AGENTS.md"` to this list.

iterations.description: Number of iterations to run for each task (default: 1).

kimi_bin.description: Override the kimi executable name/path (default is "kimi" if None).

notification.description: Desktop notification configuration for task completion.

opencode_bin.description: Override the opencode executable name/path (default is "opencode" if None).

phase_overrides.description:
Per-phase overrides for runner, model, and reasoning effort.

Allows specifying different settings for each phase (1, 2, 3).
Phase-specific values override the global agent settings.

phases.description:
Number of execution phases (1, 2, or 3).
1 = single-pass, 2 = plan+implement, 3 = plan+implement+review.

pi_bin.description: Override the pi executable name/path (default is "pi" if None).

reasoning_effort.description: Default reasoning effort (only meaningful for Codex models).

repoprompt_plan_required.description: Require RepoPrompt usage during planning (inject context_builder instructions).

repoprompt_tool_injection.description: Inject RepoPrompt tooling reminder block into prompts.

runner.description: Which harness to use by default.

runner_cli.description:
Normalized runner CLI behavior overrides (output/approval/sandbox/etc).

This is additive: existing runner-specific fields remain supported.

scan_prompt_version.description: Scan prompt version to use (v1 or v2, default: v2).

session_timeout_hours.description:
Session timeout in hours for crash recovery (default: 24).
Sessions older than this threshold are considered stale and require
explicit user confirmation to resume.

webhook.description: Webhook configuration for HTTP task event notifications.

argv.description: Direct argv execution. The first item is the program and remaining items are arguments.

enabled.description: Enable or disable the CI gate entirely.

agent.description: Agent runner defaults. Built-in runner IDs: codex, opencode, gemini, claude, cursor, kimi, pi. Plugin runner IDs are also supported as non-empty strings.

profiles.description:
Optional named profiles for quick workflow switching.

Each profile is an AgentConfig-shaped patch applied over `agent` when selected.
Profile values override base config but are overridden by CLI flags and task.agent.

project_type.description: "code" or "docs". Drives prompt defaults and small workflow decisions.

GitPublishMode.description: Git publish mode for post-run repository changes.

oneOf.description: Leave the repository dirty after queue/done updates.
Create a local commit but do not push.
Create a local commit and push it using Ralph's guarded push flow.

GitRevertMode.description: Git revert mode for handling runner/supervision errors.

LoopConfig.description: Run loop waiting configuration for daemon/continuous mode.

empty_poll_ms.description:
Poll interval in milliseconds while waiting for new tasks when queue is empty.
Default: 30000 (30 seconds). Minimum: 50.

notify_when_unblocked.description: Send notification when queue transitions from blocked to runnable.

wait_poll_ms.description:
Poll interval in milliseconds while waiting for blocked tasks to become runnable.
Default: 1000 (1 second). Minimum: 50.

wait_timeout_seconds.description: Timeout in seconds for waiting (0 = no timeout).

wait_when_blocked.description: Wait when blocked by dependencies/schedule instead of exiting.

wait_when_empty.description: Wait when queue is empty instead of exiting (continuous/daemon mode).

enabled.description:
Enable desktop notifications on task completion (default: true).
This is the legacy/compatibility field; prefer `notify_on_complete`.

notify_on_complete.description: Enable desktop notifications on task completion (default: true).

notify_on_fail.description: Enable desktop notifications on task failure (default: true).

notify_on_loop_complete.description: Enable desktop notifications when loop mode completes (default: true).

notify_on_watch_new_tasks.description: Enable desktop notifications when watch mode adds new tasks from comments (default: true).

sound_enabled.description: Enable sound alerts with notifications (default: false).

sound_path.description:
Custom sound file path (platform-specific format).
If not set, uses platform default sounds.

suppress_when_active.description: Suppress notifications when a foreground UI client is active (default: true).

max_push_attempts.description: Maximum number of push attempts before giving up.

push_backoff_ms.description: Backoff intervals in milliseconds for push retries.

workers.description: Number of workers to run concurrently when parallel mode is enabled.

workspace_retention_hours.description: Hours to retain blocked workspaces before cleanup.

workspace_root.description: Root directory for parallel workspaces (relative to repo root if not absolute).

PhaseOverrideConfig.description:
Per-phase configuration overrides for runner, model, and reasoning effort.

All fields are optional to support leaf-wise merging:
- `Some(value)` overrides the parent config
- `None` means "inherit from parent"

model.description: Model to use for this phase (overrides global agent.model)

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort for this phase (overrides global agent.reasoning_effort)

runner.description: Runner to use for this phase (overrides global agent.runner)

PhaseOverrides.description:
Phase overrides container for Phase 1/2/3 execution.

Per-phase configuration for Phase 1/2/3 execution.

Invariants/assumptions:
- Overrides are defined per phase only; there is no shared `defaults` layer inside
  `agent.phase_overrides`. Use global `agent.runner` / `agent.model` /
  `agent.reasoning_effort` for shared defaults.
- Merging is leaf-wise: `Some(value)` overrides, `None` inherits.

config.description: Opaque plugin configuration blob (passed through to the plugin).

enabled.description: Enable/disable the plugin. If None, defaults to disabled.

plugins.description: Per-plugin configuration keyed by plugin id.

QueueAgingThresholds.description:
Aging threshold configuration for `ralph queue aging`.

Controls the day thresholds for categorizing tasks by age.
Ordering invariant: warning_days < stale_days < rotten_days

rotten_days.description: Mark as rotten when age is strictly greater than this many days.

stale_days.description: Mark as stale when age is strictly greater than this many days.

warning_days.description: Warn when task age is strictly greater than this many days.

aging_thresholds.description:
Thresholds for `ralph queue aging` buckets.

Default: warning>7d, stale>14d, rotten>30d.
Ordering must satisfy: warning_days < stale_days < rotten_days.

auto_archive_terminal_after_days.description:
Auto-archive terminal tasks (done/rejected) from queue to done after this many days.

Semantics:
- None: disabled (default)
- Some(0): archive immediately when the sweep runs
- Some(N): archive when completed_at is at least N days old

The sweep runs after selected queue mutation operations (e.g., task edits and run supervision).
Tasks with missing or invalid completed_at timestamps are not moved when N > 0.

done_file.description:
Path to the JSON done archive file, relative to repo root.

Paths are intended to be repo-root relative. Parallel mode requires the
resolved path to be under the repo root (no `..`) so it can be copied
into workspace clones.

file.description:
Path to the JSON queue file, relative to repo root.

Paths are intended to be repo-root relative. Parallel mode requires the
resolved path to be under the repo root (no `..`) so it can be copied
into workspace clones.

id_width.description: Zero pad width for the numeric suffix (default: 4 -> RQ-0001).

max_dependency_depth.description: Maximum allowed dependency chain depth before warning (default: 10).

size_warning_threshold_kb.description: Warning threshold for queue file size in KB (default: 500).

task_count_warning_threshold.description: Warning threshold for number of tasks in queue (default: 500).

Runner.description: Runner id. Built-in runner IDs: codex, opencode, gemini, claude, cursor, kimi, pi. Plugin runner IDs are also supported as non-empty strings.

oneOf.description: Do not apply any approval flags; runner defaults apply.
Attempt to auto-approve edits but not all tool actions (runner-specific).
Strict safety mode. Warning: some runners may become interactive and hang.

defaults.description: Default normalized runner CLI options applied to all runners (unless overridden).

runners.description: Optional per-runner overrides, merged leaf-wise over `defaults`.

output_format.description: Desired output format for runner execution.

plan_mode.description: Desired plan/read-only behavior (when supported by the runner).

sandbox.description: Desired sandbox behavior (when supported by the runner).

verbosity.description: Desired verbosity (when supported by the runner).

oneOf.description: Newline-delimited JSON objects (required for Ralph's streaming parser).
JSON output (may not be streaming; currently treated as unsupported by Ralph execution).
Plain text output (currently treated as unsupported by Ralph execution).

RunnerRetryConfig.description:
Runner retry/backoff configuration for transient failure handling.

Controls automatic retry behavior when runner invocations fail with
transient errors (rate limits, temporary unavailability, network issues).
Distinct from webhook retry settings (`agent.webhook.retry_*`).

max_backoff_ms.description: Max backoff cap in milliseconds. Default: 30000.

ScanPromptVersion.description: Scan prompt version to use for scan operations.

oneOf.description: Version 1: Original rule-based scan prompts with fixed minimum task counts.
Version 2: Rubric-based scan prompts with quality-focused STOP CONDITION (default).

WebhookConfig.description: Webhook configuration for HTTP task event notifications.

allow_insecure_http.description: Opt-in to allow plaintext http:// webhook URLs (default: HTTPS only).

allow_private_targets.description: Opt-in to allow loopback, link-local (169.254/…), and metadata-style hosts.

events.description: Events to subscribe to (default: legacy task events only).

parallel_queue_multiplier.description:
Multiplier for queue capacity in parallel mode (default: 2.0, range: 1.0-10.0).
When running with N parallel workers, effective capacity = queue_capacity * max(1, workers * multiplier).
Set higher (e.g., 3.0) if webhook endpoint is slow or unreliable.

queue_capacity.description: Maximum number of pending webhooks in the delivery queue (default: 500, range: 10-10000).

queue_policy.description:
Backpressure policy when queue is full (default: drop_oldest).
- drop_oldest: Drop new webhooks when full (preserves existing queue contents)
- drop_new: Drop the new webhook if queue is full
- block_with_timeout: Block sender briefly (100ms), then drop if still full

retry_backoff_ms.description:
Base interval for exponential webhook retry delays in milliseconds (default: 1000, max: 30000).
Actual delays apply bounded jitter and cap at 30 seconds between attempts.

retry_count.description: Number of retry attempts for failed deliveries (default: 3, max: 10).

secret.description:
Secret key for HMAC-SHA256 signature generation.
When set, webhooks include an X-Ralph-Signature header.

WebhookEventSubscription.description:
Webhook event subscription type for config.
Each variant corresponds to a WebhookEventType, plus Wildcard for "all events".

oneOf.description: Task status changed to Doing (execution started).

WebhookQueuePolicy.description: Backpressure policy for webhook delivery queue.

oneOf.description:
Drop new webhooks when queue is full, preserving existing queue contents.
This is functionally equivalent to `drop_new` due to channel constraints.
Drop the new webhook if queue is full.
Block sender briefly, then drop if queue is still full.

observed_at.description: When this blocking condition was observed (RFC3339 UTC), for staleness and automation.
When this blocking condition was observed (RFC3339 UTC), for staleness and automation.
When this blocking condition was observed (RFC3339 UTC), for staleness and automation.

MachineParallelLifecycleCounts.description: Worker counts by lifecycle for `machine run parallel-status` (document v3+).

PhaseOverrideConfig.description:
Per-phase configuration overrides for runner, model, and reasoning effort.

All fields are optional to support leaf-wise merging:
- `Some(value)` overrides the parent config
- `None` means "inherit from parent"

model.description: Model to use for this phase (overrides global agent.model)

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort for this phase (overrides global agent.reasoning_effort)

runner.description: Runner to use for this phase (overrides global agent.runner)

PhaseOverrides.description:
Phase overrides container for Phase 1/2/3 execution.

Per-phase configuration for Phase 1/2/3 execution.

Invariants/assumptions:
- Overrides are defined per phase only; there is no shared `defaults` layer inside
  `agent.phase_overrides`. Use global `agent.runner` / `agent.model` /
  `agent.reasoning_effort` for shared defaults.
- Merging is leaf-wise: `Some(value)` overrides, `None` inherits.

Runner.description: Runner id. Built-in runner IDs: codex, opencode, gemini, claude, cursor, kimi, pi. Plugin runner IDs are also supported as non-empty strings.

oneOf.description: Do not apply any approval flags; runner defaults apply.
Attempt to auto-approve edits but not all tool actions (runner-specific).
Strict safety mode. Warning: some runners may become interactive and hang.

output_format.description: Desired output format for runner execution.

plan_mode.description: Desired plan/read-only behavior (when supported by the runner).

sandbox.description: Desired sandbox behavior (when supported by the runner).

verbosity.description: Desired verbosity (when supported by the runner).

oneOf.description: Newline-delimited JSON objects (required for Ralph's streaming parser).
JSON output (may not be streaming; currently treated as unsupported by Ralph execution).
Plain text output (currently treated as unsupported by Ralph execution).

actual_minutes.description:
Actual time spent on this task in minutes.
Optional; set manually or computed from started_at to completed_at.

blocks.description:
Task IDs that this task blocks (must be Done/Rejected before blocked tasks can run).
Semantically different from depends_on: blocks is "I prevent X" vs depends_on "I need X".

created_at.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamps as strings to keep the contract tool-agnostic.

custom_fields.description:
Custom user-defined fields (key-value pairs for extensibility).
Values may be written as string/number/boolean; Ralph coerces them to strings when loading.

depends_on.description: Task IDs that this task depends on (must be Done or Rejected before this task can run).

description.description: Detailed description of the task's context, goal, purpose, and desired outcome.

duplicates.description:
Task ID that this task duplicates (if any).
Singular reference, not a list.

estimated_minutes.description:
Estimated time to complete this task in minutes.
Optional; used for planning and estimation accuracy tracking.

parent_id.description: Parent task ID if this is a subtask (child-to-parent reference).

relates_to.description:
Task IDs that this task relates to (loose coupling, no execution constraint).
Bidirectional awareness but no execution constraint.

request.description: Original human request that created the task (Task Builder / Scan).

scheduled_start.description: RFC3339 timestamp when the task should become runnable (optional scheduling).

started_at.description:
RFC3339 UTC timestamp when work on this task actually started.

Invariants:
- Must be RFC3339 UTC (Z) if set.
- Should be set when transitioning into `doing` (see status policy).

followup_reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort override for follow-up iterations (iterations > 1).

iterations.description: Number of iterations to run for this task (overrides config).

model_effort.description: Per-task reasoning effort override for Codex models. Default falls back to config.

phase_overrides.description: Optional per-phase runner/model/effort overrides for this task.

phases.description: Number of execution phases for this task (1, 2, or 3), overriding config defaults.

runner_cli.description:
Optional normalized runner CLI overrides for this task.

This is intended to express runner behavior intent (output/approval/sandbox/etc)
without embedding runner-specific flag syntax into the queue.

TaskPriority.description: Task priority used for queue ordering and display.

observed_at.description: When this blocking condition was observed (RFC3339 UTC), for staleness and automation.
When this blocking condition was observed (RFC3339 UTC), for staleness and automation.
When this blocking condition was observed (RFC3339 UTC), for staleness and automation.
When this blocking condition was observed (RFC3339 UTC), for staleness and automation.
When this blocking condition was observed (RFC3339 UTC), for staleness and automation.

AgentConfig.description: Agent runner defaults. Built-in runner IDs: codex, opencode, gemini, claude, cursor, kimi, pi. Plugin runner IDs are also supported as non-empty strings.

claude_bin.description: Override the claude executable name/path (default is "claude" if None).

claude_permission_mode.description:
Claude permission mode for tool and edit approval.
AcceptEdits: auto-approves file edits only
BypassPermissions: skip all permission prompts (YOLO mode)

codex_bin.description: Override the codex executable name/path (default is "codex" if None).

cursor_bin.description:
Override the cursor agent executable name/path (default is "agent" if None).

NOTE: Cursor's runner binary name is `agent` (not `cursor`).

followup_reasoning_effort.description:
Reasoning effort override for follow-up iterations (iterations > 1).
Only meaningful for Codex models.

gemini_bin.description: Override the gemini executable name/path (default is "gemini" if None).

git_publish_mode.description: Post-run git publication behavior after successful runs.

git_revert_mode.description: Controls automatic git revert behavior when runner or supervision errors occur.

instruction_files.description:
Additional instruction files to inject at the top of every prompt sent to runner CLIs.

Paths may be absolute, `~/`-prefixed, or repo-root relative. Each list entry must be a
non-empty path; blank or whitespace-only strings are rejected during config validation.
Missing files are treated as configuration errors. To include repo-local AGENTS.md, add
`"AGENTS.md"` to this list.

iterations.description: Number of iterations to run for each task (default: 1).

kimi_bin.description: Override the kimi executable name/path (default is "kimi" if None).

notification.description: Desktop notification configuration for task completion.

opencode_bin.description: Override the opencode executable name/path (default is "opencode" if None).

phase_overrides.description:
Per-phase overrides for runner, model, and reasoning effort.

Allows specifying different settings for each phase (1, 2, 3).
Phase-specific values override the global agent settings.

phases.description:
Number of execution phases (1, 2, or 3).
1 = single-pass, 2 = plan+implement, 3 = plan+implement+review.

pi_bin.description: Override the pi executable name/path (default is "pi" if None).

reasoning_effort.description: Default reasoning effort (only meaningful for Codex models).

repoprompt_plan_required.description: Require RepoPrompt usage during planning (inject context_builder instructions).

repoprompt_tool_injection.description: Inject RepoPrompt tooling reminder block into prompts.

runner.description: Which harness to use by default.

runner_cli.description:
Normalized runner CLI behavior overrides (output/approval/sandbox/etc).

This is additive: existing runner-specific fields remain supported.

scan_prompt_version.description: Scan prompt version to use (v1 or v2, default: v2).

session_timeout_hours.description:
Session timeout in hours for crash recovery (default: 24).
Sessions older than this threshold are considered stale and require
explicit user confirmation to resume.

webhook.description: Webhook configuration for HTTP task event notifications.

argv.description: Direct argv execution. The first item is the program and remaining items are arguments.

enabled.description: Enable or disable the CI gate entirely.

agent.description: Agent runner defaults. Built-in runner IDs: codex, opencode, gemini, claude, cursor, kimi, pi. Plugin runner IDs are also supported as non-empty strings.

profiles.description:
Optional named profiles for quick workflow switching.

Each profile is an AgentConfig-shaped patch applied over `agent` when selected.
Profile values override base config but are overridden by CLI flags and task.agent.

project_type.description: "code" or "docs". Drives prompt defaults and small workflow decisions.

GitPublishMode.description: Git publish mode for post-run repository changes.

oneOf.description: Leave the repository dirty after queue/done updates.
Create a local commit but do not push.
Create a local commit and push it using Ralph's guarded push flow.

GitRevertMode.description: Git revert mode for handling runner/supervision errors.

LoopConfig.description: Run loop waiting configuration for daemon/continuous mode.

empty_poll_ms.description:
Poll interval in milliseconds while waiting for new tasks when queue is empty.
Default: 30000 (30 seconds). Minimum: 50.

notify_when_unblocked.description: Send notification when queue transitions from blocked to runnable.

wait_poll_ms.description:
Poll interval in milliseconds while waiting for blocked tasks to become runnable.
Default: 1000 (1 second). Minimum: 50.

wait_timeout_seconds.description: Timeout in seconds for waiting (0 = no timeout).

wait_when_blocked.description: Wait when blocked by dependencies/schedule instead of exiting.

wait_when_empty.description: Wait when queue is empty instead of exiting (continuous/daemon mode).

enabled.description:
Enable desktop notifications on task completion (default: true).
This is the legacy/compatibility field; prefer `notify_on_complete`.

notify_on_complete.description: Enable desktop notifications on task completion (default: true).

notify_on_fail.description: Enable desktop notifications on task failure (default: true).

notify_on_loop_complete.description: Enable desktop notifications when loop mode completes (default: true).

notify_on_watch_new_tasks.description: Enable desktop notifications when watch mode adds new tasks from comments (default: true).

sound_enabled.description: Enable sound alerts with notifications (default: false).

sound_path.description:
Custom sound file path (platform-specific format).
If not set, uses platform default sounds.

suppress_when_active.description: Suppress notifications when a foreground UI client is active (default: true).

max_push_attempts.description: Maximum number of push attempts before giving up.

push_backoff_ms.description: Backoff intervals in milliseconds for push retries.

workers.description: Number of workers to run concurrently when parallel mode is enabled.

workspace_retention_hours.description: Hours to retain blocked workspaces before cleanup.

workspace_root.description: Root directory for parallel workspaces (relative to repo root if not absolute).

PhaseOverrideConfig.description:
Per-phase configuration overrides for runner, model, and reasoning effort.

All fields are optional to support leaf-wise merging:
- `Some(value)` overrides the parent config
- `None` means "inherit from parent"

model.description: Model to use for this phase (overrides global agent.model)

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort for this phase (overrides global agent.reasoning_effort)

runner.description: Runner to use for this phase (overrides global agent.runner)

PhaseOverrides.description:
Phase overrides container for Phase 1/2/3 execution.

Per-phase configuration for Phase 1/2/3 execution.

Invariants/assumptions:
- Overrides are defined per phase only; there is no shared `defaults` layer inside
  `agent.phase_overrides`. Use global `agent.runner` / `agent.model` /
  `agent.reasoning_effort` for shared defaults.
- Merging is leaf-wise: `Some(value)` overrides, `None` inherits.

config.description: Opaque plugin configuration blob (passed through to the plugin).

enabled.description: Enable/disable the plugin. If None, defaults to disabled.

plugins.description: Per-plugin configuration keyed by plugin id.

QueueAgingThresholds.description:
Aging threshold configuration for `ralph queue aging`.

Controls the day thresholds for categorizing tasks by age.
Ordering invariant: warning_days < stale_days < rotten_days

rotten_days.description: Mark as rotten when age is strictly greater than this many days.

stale_days.description: Mark as stale when age is strictly greater than this many days.

warning_days.description: Warn when task age is strictly greater than this many days.

aging_thresholds.description:
Thresholds for `ralph queue aging` buckets.

Default: warning>7d, stale>14d, rotten>30d.
Ordering must satisfy: warning_days < stale_days < rotten_days.

auto_archive_terminal_after_days.description:
Auto-archive terminal tasks (done/rejected) from queue to done after this many days.

Semantics:
- None: disabled (default)
- Some(0): archive immediately when the sweep runs
- Some(N): archive when completed_at is at least N days old

The sweep runs after selected queue mutation operations (e.g., task edits and run supervision).
Tasks with missing or invalid completed_at timestamps are not moved when N > 0.

done_file.description:
Path to the JSON done archive file, relative to repo root.

Paths are intended to be repo-root relative. Parallel mode requires the
resolved path to be under the repo root (no `..`) so it can be copied
into workspace clones.

file.description:
Path to the JSON queue file, relative to repo root.

Paths are intended to be repo-root relative. Parallel mode requires the
resolved path to be under the repo root (no `..`) so it can be copied
into workspace clones.

id_width.description: Zero pad width for the numeric suffix (default: 4 -> RQ-0001).

max_dependency_depth.description: Maximum allowed dependency chain depth before warning (default: 10).

size_warning_threshold_kb.description: Warning threshold for queue file size in KB (default: 500).

task_count_warning_threshold.description: Warning threshold for number of tasks in queue (default: 500).

Runner.description: Runner id. Built-in runner IDs: codex, opencode, gemini, claude, cursor, kimi, pi. Plugin runner IDs are also supported as non-empty strings.

oneOf.description: Do not apply any approval flags; runner defaults apply.
Attempt to auto-approve edits but not all tool actions (runner-specific).
Strict safety mode. Warning: some runners may become interactive and hang.

defaults.description: Default normalized runner CLI options applied to all runners (unless overridden).

runners.description: Optional per-runner overrides, merged leaf-wise over `defaults`.

output_format.description: Desired output format for runner execution.

plan_mode.description: Desired plan/read-only behavior (when supported by the runner).

sandbox.description: Desired sandbox behavior (when supported by the runner).

verbosity.description: Desired verbosity (when supported by the runner).

oneOf.description: Newline-delimited JSON objects (required for Ralph's streaming parser).
JSON output (may not be streaming; currently treated as unsupported by Ralph execution).
Plain text output (currently treated as unsupported by Ralph execution).

RunnerRetryConfig.description:
Runner retry/backoff configuration for transient failure handling.

Controls automatic retry behavior when runner invocations fail with
transient errors (rate limits, temporary unavailability, network issues).
Distinct from webhook retry settings (`agent.webhook.retry_*`).

max_backoff_ms.description: Max backoff cap in milliseconds. Default: 30000.

ScanPromptVersion.description: Scan prompt version to use for scan operations.

oneOf.description: Version 1: Original rule-based scan prompts with fixed minimum task counts.
Version 2: Rubric-based scan prompts with quality-focused STOP CONDITION (default).

actual_minutes.description:
Actual time spent on this task in minutes.
Optional; set manually or computed from started_at to completed_at.

blocks.description:
Task IDs that this task blocks (must be Done/Rejected before blocked tasks can run).
Semantically different from depends_on: blocks is "I prevent X" vs depends_on "I need X".

created_at.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamps as strings to keep the contract tool-agnostic.

custom_fields.description:
Custom user-defined fields (key-value pairs for extensibility).
Values may be written as string/number/boolean; Ralph coerces them to strings when loading.

depends_on.description: Task IDs that this task depends on (must be Done or Rejected before this task can run).

description.description: Detailed description of the task's context, goal, purpose, and desired outcome.

duplicates.description:
Task ID that this task duplicates (if any).
Singular reference, not a list.

estimated_minutes.description:
Estimated time to complete this task in minutes.
Optional; used for planning and estimation accuracy tracking.

parent_id.description: Parent task ID if this is a subtask (child-to-parent reference).

relates_to.description:
Task IDs that this task relates to (loose coupling, no execution constraint).
Bidirectional awareness but no execution constraint.

request.description: Original human request that created the task (Task Builder / Scan).

scheduled_start.description: RFC3339 timestamp when the task should become runnable (optional scheduling).

started_at.description:
RFC3339 UTC timestamp when work on this task actually started.

Invariants:
- Must be RFC3339 UTC (Z) if set.
- Should be set when transitioning into `doing` (see status policy).

followup_reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort override for follow-up iterations (iterations > 1).

iterations.description: Number of iterations to run for this task (overrides config).

model_effort.description: Per-task reasoning effort override for Codex models. Default falls back to config.

phase_overrides.description: Optional per-phase runner/model/effort overrides for this task.

phases.description: Number of execution phases for this task (1, 2, or 3), overriding config defaults.

runner_cli.description:
Optional normalized runner CLI overrides for this task.

This is intended to express runner behavior intent (output/approval/sandbox/etc)
without embedding runner-specific flag syntax into the queue.

TaskPriority.description: Task priority used for queue ordering and display.

WebhookConfig.description: Webhook configuration for HTTP task event notifications.

allow_insecure_http.description: Opt-in to allow plaintext http:// webhook URLs (default: HTTPS only).

allow_private_targets.description: Opt-in to allow loopback, link-local (169.254/…), and metadata-style hosts.

events.description: Events to subscribe to (default: legacy task events only).

parallel_queue_multiplier.description:
Multiplier for queue capacity in parallel mode (default: 2.0, range: 1.0-10.0).
When running with N parallel workers, effective capacity = queue_capacity * max(1, workers * multiplier).
Set higher (e.g., 3.0) if webhook endpoint is slow or unreliable.

queue_capacity.description: Maximum number of pending webhooks in the delivery queue (default: 500, range: 10-10000).

queue_policy.description:
Backpressure policy when queue is full (default: drop_oldest).
- drop_oldest: Drop new webhooks when full (preserves existing queue contents)
- drop_new: Drop the new webhook if queue is full
- block_with_timeout: Block sender briefly (100ms), then drop if still full

retry_backoff_ms.description:
Base interval for exponential webhook retry delays in milliseconds (default: 1000, max: 30000).
Actual delays apply bounded jitter and cap at 30 seconds between attempts.

retry_count.description: Number of retry attempts for failed deliveries (default: 3, max: 10).

secret.description:
Secret key for HMAC-SHA256 signature generation.
When set, webhooks include an X-Ralph-Signature header.

WebhookEventSubscription.description:
Webhook event subscription type for config.
Each variant corresponds to a WebhookEventType, plus Wildcard for "all events".

oneOf.description: Task status changed to Doing (execution started).

WebhookQueuePolicy.description: Backpressure policy for webhook delivery queue.

oneOf.description:
Drop new webhooks when queue is full, preserving existing queue contents.
This is functionally equivalent to `drop_new` due to channel constraints.
Drop the new webhook if queue is full.
Block sender briefly, then drop if queue is still full.
